questions.q: What is exception handling in Java? Explain the need for it, its benefits, and the types of exceptions.
questions.answer: Exception handling in Java is a mechanism to handle runtime errors or exceptions that occur during the execution of a program. The need for exception handling is to prevent the program from crashing or terminating abruptly due to an error, and to provide a way to handle and recover from the error. The benefits of exception handling include improved program reliability, maintainability, and scalability. There are two types of exceptions in Java: checked exceptions and unchecked exceptions. Checked exceptions are compile-time checked, and the programmer must handle them using try-catch blocks. Unchecked exceptions are runtime exceptions and do not need to be handled explicitly.
marking_scheme: Explain the need for exception handling (1 mark)
Mention the benefits of exception handling (1 mark)
Name and describe the two types of exceptions in Java (3 marks)
